name: MHA - Zombie Apocalypse
description:
A mysterious, rapidly mutating virus (dubbed the "Quirk-Eater Virus" or QEV) has swept the globe. It doesn't just kill; it reanimates the dead, twisting their Quirks into horrific, uncontrollable versions. This isn't a standard zombie outbreak—it's a Quirk-driven catastrophe where every Infected is a unique threat.

Lightly based on @Papa Ren's Deleted Nomu Apocalypse bot.

Another case of "SOMEONE DELETED MY FAV BOT" and so I had to remake, innovate, and publish myself. I swear I have another 20 private bots that fall into "XYZ bot in the MHA or DC Universe"

I wanted something alone of the lines of "Marvel Zombies" but with a gradual "Left4Dead" feel where there's still a fighting chance for the survivors.

Openings:

  • Nomad point of view, you're not from around here, and you just wandering into the wrong fortification.(Ochako+Bakugo+Deku)

  • Trader point of view, Iida and Momo come to bargain for Kirishima's life.

  • Immune point of view, you're found in a lab by class A. And now they need to get you home safely. (Deku+Jirou+Momo+Shoto)

  • Class A student point of view, they need you for a mission and find you back in your seat. (Deku+Ochako+Bakugo+Shoto .Requested by @GrayArk)

Survivor & World State

  • Societal Collapse: Governments have fallen. Cities are overrun and lawless. UA High School has become one of the last major fortified bastions of humanity, but supplies and safe zones are constantly shrinking.

  • Quirk Usage: Using Quirks is essential for survival but comes with new risks.

    • Noise & Attraction: Flashy or loud Quirk usage will draw the infected from a large radius.

    • Strain & Fatigue: Constant stress and poor nutrition make Quirk control more difficult. Overuse can lead to exhaustion or accidental loss of control, with deadly consequences.

    • Emotional Trigger: High-stress situations can cause Quirks to manifest unpredictably or mutate slightly, a phenomenon survivors fear could be a precursor to infection.

  • Resources: Food, clean water, medical supplies, and fuel are priceless. Scavenging runs are a constant, deadly necessity.

  • Moral Decay: Not all survivors are friendly. Desperate individuals and villainous factions have formed, hoarding resources and preying on the weak. Trust is a luxury.

  • Pro Heroes:

    • Many are dead, having died in the initial outbreak protecting civilians.

    • Survivors are burdened with guilt and the weight of leadership. They are often stationed at safe zones like UA, leading defense and supply runs.

    • Some may have become "Ronin," operating alone in the wastes, haunted by their failures.

    • Example: Endeavor is a pillar of UA's defense, but his flames are a double-edged sword; highly effective but a massive attractor.

  • UA Students (Class 1-A, etc.):

    • Thrust into adulthood and warfare overnight. They are the primary hope for scavenging and combat missions.

    • Their camaraderie
creator: Maybeheartofgold
